title: Fresh-slick](https://www.kaggle.com/code/ferariz/f1-pit-stops-does-the-model-know-when-to-pit%22]},{%22title%22:%22Fresh-slick) option richness after a next-lap stop
summary: Extend next-lap fresh-slick feasibility with the number of viable slick choices, per-compound margins, the softest viable slick, and whether a different slick can finish.
rationale: Variants adding option richness produced very similar strong predictions in the run, suggesting the mechanism is stable but somewhat duplicate-prone. Strategy flexibility near the final-stop window is distinct from a single any-can-finish flag.
implementation_hint: For fresh SOFT, MEDIUM, and HARD after a next-lap stop, add per-compound finish margins, option count, best and worst viable margin, softest viable ordinal, and a different-from-current-slick viability flag. Compare this against the simpler next-stop-feasibility block.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with medium-high confidence; expected to help ranking around final-stop boundaries.
risk: Avoid generating many aliases of the same option-count logic. Check public/validation consistency with grouped validation, not a single random holdout.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
